title: The Adventure of Black Peter
synopsis: Captain Peter Carey, known as Black Peter for his violent temper, is found harpooned through the chest in his isolated outhouse in Woodman's Lee. A young stockbroker named John Neligan is found lurking near the scene, apparently connected to a missing notebook, and is arrested. Holmes works the case in disguise under the alias Captain Basil.
solution: Patrick Cairns, a harpooner who had served on the Sea Unicorn with Black Peter, killed Carey when Carey refused to share the proceeds of a blackmail plot over the Sea Unicorn's log; Holmes baits a trap by advertising for harpooners seeking Arctic employment, luring Cairns to Baker Street where he is arrested.
notable_elements: Holmes tests whether a man could drive a harpoon through a pig's carcass to establish the strength required
Holmes operates in disguise as Captain Basil for days before Watson realises
The Sea Unicorn log documents a murder and insurance fraud Carey had been profiting from
Neligan is cleared — he was searching for his father's bonds that Black Peter had stolen years earlier
